game_name: Skate or Die 2: The Search for Double Trouble

danger_signals.visual_form: Villain character sprite and thugs in warehouse stage

danger_signals.meaning: Authority figure enforcing skateboarding ban; conflict danger
Bureaucratic obstacle; environmental threat to skateboarding culture
Spatial disorientation and danger from enemy encounters in confined space

safety_signals.visual_form: Friendly NPC sprite offering skateboard upgrades
Girlfriend character sprite and window appearance at Double Trouble
safety_signals.meaning: Narrative motivation; reward signal when scoring well

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Skateboard equipment; paintball gun as distinctive weapon
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Other skaters in levels act as minor antagonists/obstacles
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Street locations (Elwood streets, half-pipes, shopping mall); casual youth culture spaces

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mayor's office, construction equipment, security guard uniform
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mayor's wife, construction worker, security guard as visual representatives
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: City hall, warehouse headquarters, bureaucratic spaces

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Icepick character as leader; thug NPCs as gang members

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Skateboarding is banned by mayor's decree
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mayor's wife as antagonist in stage 1; authority figures present throughout early game
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hero must confront authority figures; skateboarding activity restricted narratively

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Construction worker blocks access; demolition scenario in cutscene
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hero takes job as delivery boy to earn permit money; must navigate mall stage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Blueprint pages as collectible items scattered across stage 3
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hero must collect all pages before they blow away; environmental timer pressure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: CJ absence from active gameplay; Icepick's warehouse becomes final stage location
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hero navigates warehouse maze to rescue CJ; final boss confrontation with Icepick

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Double Trouble half-pipe stage becomes available; two-screen halfpipe environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can perform tricks freely; CJ appears in background window when scoring well (points doubling)

environmental_storytelling.example: Streets (skateboarding ban conflict) → shopping mall (economic solution via work) → beach (accident consequences) → warehouse (final confrontation)
Mayor's wife and construction worker represent authority; Rodney and Lester as grassroots community mentors; Icepick as criminal outsider

environmental_storytelling.technique: Environmental hazard variety indicating stage themes
environmental_storytelling.example: Stage 1: street obstacles and NPC skaters; Stage 2: shoppers and security; Stage 3: wildlife (crabs, seagulls) and roller skaters; Stage 4: warehouse maze layout
Collectible items (CDs, tapes, food) appear after defeating enemies/skaters, indicating scavenging reward loop
Opening cutscene establishes town setting and conflict; inter-stage cutscenes show consequences and plot progression

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Armed with paintball gun and throwing weapons
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Combat-ready; can defeat enemies and retrieve items
Uses authority to ban skateboarding; confronts hero in stage 1
Appears in stages to sell skateboards and offer trades for collected items
Elusive; must be caught to receive trick training; requires goodies as payment
Blocks half-pipe access; explains bureaucratic requirement for permit
Moves through shopping mall as stage hazard during delivery mission
Initiates conflict in opening; kidnaps CJ; serves as final boss in warehouse
Appears in background window during successful trick runs; doubles points when visible

notes: Skate or Die 2 uses world-state progression to communicate narrative tension: each stage represents escalating conflict and bureaucratic obstacles. Visual communication relies heavily on NPC character sprites to indicate faction alignment (friendly mentors vs. authority antagonists). The warehouse stage uses spatial maze design to communicate danger escalation. Enemy variety (skaters, shoppers, wildlife, thugs) indicates stage thematic progression. The 'Double Trouble' halfpipe finale serves as visual reward manifestation—achievement of the narrative goal. CJ's appearance mechanics (point doubling during tricks) directly link romantic reward to player performance, creating visual feedback loop.
